# Clarity Track — Gold-Standard Artifact-Track Example

**Value promise:** By the end you will have a career statement, a dream job, and an AI career coach that knows you.

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Slug | `clarity` |
| Type | artifact-track |
| Steps | 12 (Step 0–11) |
| Total substeps | ~135 |
| Purpose | Build Personal Profile — Train AI Career Coach |
| Completion output | Career Statement, Dream Job, Career Path, fully primed coaching context |

---

## Track at a Glance

| Step | What it collects | Output |
|------|-----------------|--------|
| **0 — Onboarding** | Name, age, gender, location, education, work experience, career clarity level, job-acquisition confidence | Baseline profile; coach anchored to clarity + confidence levels |
| **1 — Discover Your Personality** | 42 binary-choice questions across 5 contexts (Professional, Social, Personal Growth, Problem Solving, Work Environment) | Multidimensional personality profile; first coaching chat |
| **2 — Strengths** | 6 free-response reflection questions (what's easy, energizing, praised, natural, flow-inducing, team-fulfilling); AI generates archetypes | Top 3 named strength archetypes selected by student |
| **3 — Inspirations** | 6 free-response questions (world problem, inspiring industries, aligned companies, role models, engaging topics, if money didn't matter); AI generates archetypes | Top 3 named inspiration archetypes selected by student |
| **4 — Work Environment** | 6 free-response questions (setting, flexibility, culture, physical space, schedule, stress tolerance) | AI-generated ideal work environment profile (generate, not select) |
| **5 — Living Environment** | 6 free-response questions (urban/suburban/rural, climate, scene, distance from home, transport, nature proximity) | AI-generated ideal living environment profile (generate, not select) |
| **6 — Values** | Select 12 from 34 → narrow to 8 → narrow to 6; guided by reflection prompts at each cut | Top 6 core values arrived at through forced prioritization |
| **7 — Financial Needs** | 6 monthly cost inputs (housing, food, transport, utilities, debt, misc) | Calculated monthly + annual income target; hard constraint on job recommendations |
| **8 — Dream Job** | Familiarity preference, education openness, optional sector; AI generates 5 profile-matched job cards | One selected dream job; north star for the rest of the track |
| **9 — Dream Job Requirements** | AI generates 5-category requirements breakdown; student marks which they already meet | Gap map: education, core skills, experience, soft skills, nice-to-haves |
| **10 — Career Statement** | No new input — pure synthesis of Steps 0–10 | AI-generated personalized career statement; student refines in chat |
| **11 — Coach Chat** | Open conversation | Full-context coaching activated; 5 conversation starters surfaced |

---

## Patterns This Track Demonstrates

### 1. Generation-then-select
Used in Steps 2 (strengths), 3 (inspirations), and 8 (dream jobs). The student first answers free-response questions in their own words; the AI synthesizes those answers into a set of named archetypes or job cards; the student then selects from AI-generated options rather than authoring from scratch. This sidesteps the "list your strengths" blind spot — people can't name what feels invisible — and produces student-recognized options rather than self-reported noise. The AI earns visible value at the moment of generation; the student retains agency at the moment of selection.

### 2. Progressive narrowing (Values: 34 → 12 → 8 → 6)
Step 6 never asks "what are your values?" It presents 34 options and applies three forced cuts, each guided by a different reflection question (What defines me? / What influences my decisions? / What would I never compromise?). Each cut requires genuine prioritization under simulated pressure. The result reveals what a student actually prioritizes when tradeoffs are required — a far more useful signal than an aspirational self-report.

### 3. GENERATE substeps for environment profiles
Steps 4 (Work Environment) and 5 (Living Environment) use a GENERATE substep — the AI writes a descriptive profile from the student's free-response answers and displays it for review. The student doesn't select from options; they review a synthesized portrait. This works for contextual preferences that resist category labels: "high-stakes, deadline-driven teams" is more precise and more resonant than any dropdown option.

### 4. Celebration arcs per step
Every major step ends with a celebration screen that summarizes what was captured ("What we learned about you"). At 135 substeps, visible progress markers are load-bearing for completion rates. The summary format also demonstrates immediately that data is being captured and used — reducing the "why are you asking this?" friction that kills mid-track drop-off.

### 5. Profile-as-coach-context
The track is not a questionnaire — it is a coaching intake. Every piece of data collected is explicitly injected into the AI coaching context so that by Step 11, the coach knows the student's personality, strengths, inspirations, environments, values, financial floor, dream job, skill gaps, and career statement. Coaching chat substeps appear after each major section — not as UX filler but as the first real coaching interactions, triggered at exactly the right moment (just after the student has learned something new about themselves).

---

*Source: Clarity Track Student Experience Documentation — artifact-track reference for the NSLS Builder Toolkit track-design skill.*
